simple_en: A Fixed Deposit is a way to invest money in a bank for a fixed time and earn guaranteed interest
analogy: like giving your money to the bank and getting extra in return
reassurance: It is one of the safest investment options

simple_en: Banks give you extra money called interest for keeping your money with them
analogy: like rent you earn for letting someone use your money
reassurance: Interest is fixed so returns are predictable

simple_en: You can choose how long you want to keep money in an FD
reassurance: Longer duration usually gives better returns

simple_en: FDs are generally safe and insured up to ₹5 lakh per bank
analogy: like having insurance protection on your money
example: Even if a bank fails, ₹5 lakh is protected
reassurance: That’s why many people trust FDs

simple_en: Interest earned from FD is taxable and bank may deduct tax in advance
You can break FD before time but may get lower interest
example: 7% FD may give 6% if broken early

simple_en: Different banks offer different interest rates on FD
analogy: like different shops offering different prices
reassurance: Comparing banks helps you earn more

simple_en: FD gives safe but lower returns, mutual funds give higher but risky returns
analogy: FD is safe road, mutual fund is fast highway with risk
reassurance: Choose based on your risk level

simple_en: You can open an FD online or by visiting a bank
analogy: like opening a savings account but simpler
example: Mobile banking apps allow FD in minutes

simple_en: People aged 60+ get higher interest rates on FD
analogy: like a bonus benefit for seniors

simple_en: Cumulative FD pays at the end, non-cumulative pays regularly
example: Choose based on income vs saving goal
reassurance: Both are useful depending on need
